page: Microsoft Azure D96ds_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Ddsv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8573C processor operating at 4.2 GHz. The system includes 384.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 4.0 GB per core allocation. It is equipped with 5669 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage. The processor architecture is x86_64, incorporating advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE, AMX_INT8) for accelerated matrix operations, and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core and memory performance. The server achieved a top-tier PassMark CPU score of 86698.13 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 21967.0. Multi-threaded decompression via bzip3 reached a top-tier speed of 507.5 GB/sec, while static web serving throughput peaked at 116.39 GB/sec. Memory bandwidth is robust, measuring 257.11 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 107.03 GB/sec for large-block write operations. For database workloads, the instance processed 107.53 million Redis SET operations per second and scored in the top tier for PassMark database operations. Large language model inference benchmarks show strong prompt processing speeds, reaching 282.63 tokens/sec on a 7B model and 35.03 tokens/sec on a 70B model, though text generation speeds are more moderate.

From a resource density perspective, the D96ds_v6 offers a balanced ratio of compute to memory alongside a substantial local NVMe storage allocation, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for high-throughput workloads that do not require dedicated GPU acceleration. The absence of a GPU limits its utility for heavy deep learning training, but its integrated AMX instructions provide a viable alternative for CPU-based inference. This server is highly suited for relational databases, high-traffic web serving, software compilation, and multi-threaded data processing tasks.
description: The Microsoft Azure D96ds_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Ddsv6 family, powered by a 96-vCPU Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz. It features 384.0 GB of RAM, 5669 GB of local storage, and supports Premium Storage. Built on the x86_64 architecture, the processor supports AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core performance, with a PassMark CPU score of 86698.13 and a static web serving throughput of 116.39 GB/sec. Database operations are highly efficient, achieving 107.53 million Redis SET operations per second. While lacking a dedicated GPU, its high core count and memory bandwidth make it highly cost-efficient for enterprise databases, software compilation, multi-threaded data processing, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D96ds_v6 is a 96-vCPU general-purpose server featuring an Intel Xeon processor, 384 GB RAM, and 5669 GB local storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D96ds_v6 is a 96-vCPU general-purpose server with 384 GB RAM, 5669 GB local storage, and an Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A high-core general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, massive local storage, and advanced matrix extensions for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor at 4.2 GHz.
384.0 GB of system memory and 5669 GB of local storage.
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated matrix and vector math.
Top-tier multi-core performance and high-throughput static web serving.
Optimized for enterprise databases, software compilation, and CPU-based inference.
